nodes.context: Camorra clan operating in Pianura, Naples, involved in cement, construction, extortion, drugs, and murders.
Camorra clan that split from Clan Lago and fought for control of drug dealing and extortion in Pianura.
Camorra clan allied with Contino, fought Clan Lago for control of drug dealing and extortion in Pianura.
Camorra alliance that supported Clan Marfella against Clan Lago.
Camorra clan from Ponticelli, whose splinter groups supported Clan Marfella against Clan Lago.
Camorra organization; Pietro Lago was indicated as a lieutenant of Casalesi boss Antonio Bardellino within it.
Camorra clan; Antonio Bardellino was its historical boss, and Pietro Lago was his lieutenant for Pianura and Soccavo.

edges.evidence_quote: Gli inizi degli anni novanta e i primi anni del decennio successivo hanno visto il clan Lago contrapporsi in una violenta guerra dapprima contro il clan Contino
edges.context: Clan Lago fought a violent war against Clan Contino for control of drug dealing and extortion.

edges.evidence_quote: successivamente, contro il clan Marfella per il controllo delle piazze di spaccio e delle estorsioni della zona di Pianura
edges.context: Clan Lago fought a violent war against Clan Marfella for control of drug dealing and extortion.

edges.evidence_quote: spalleggiato anche dall' Alleanza di Secondigliano
edges.context: Clan Marfella was supported by the Alleanza di Secondigliano in its conflict with Clan Lago.

edges.evidence_quote: dagli scissionisti del clan Sarno di Ponticelli
edges.context: Splinter groups of Clan Sarno supported Clan Marfella against Clan Lago.

edges.evidence_quote: Coinvolto nella maxi-inchiesta del 1983 contro la Nuova Famiglia
edges.context: Pietro Lago was involved in the 1983 maxi-investigation against Nuova Famiglia, indicating association.

edges.evidence_quote: Lago venne indicato nei rapporti giudiziari come luogotenente, per Pianura e Soccavo , del capo storico dei Casalesi , Antonio Bardellino
edges.context: Pietro Lago was a lieutenant of Casalesi boss Antonio Bardellino, indicating a subordinate relationship.
